review.summary: The series is close, but patch 2 still leaves two touched migration error messages inconsistent with the actual validation logic.

stages.A: Patch 1 and patch 3 match their stated intent, but patch 2 does not fully fix the touched error messages because two ranges/messages remain inaccurate after the rename cleanup.
stages.B: No execution-flow bugs were evident in the behavioral change, but two validation error strings still contradict the conditions that trigger them.
stages.C: No resource lifecycle, error-propagation, or concurrency regressions are apparent in the shown changes.
stages.D: No new guest-input, DMA, MMIO, or migration-compatibility security issues are introduced by this series.
stages.E: After deduplication, two concrete minor findings remain, both in patch 2's touched error-message paths.

findings.title: announce-step error text still advertises an invalid lower bound
findings.description: This patch claims to fix parameter references in error messages, but the touched announce-step message remains inconsistent with the validation it reports. The check rejects any value below 1, yet the emitted text says the valid range is 0 to 10000. A concrete trigger is QMP migrate-set-parameters with announce-step=0: migrate_params_check() takes this branch and returns an error that incorrectly tells the user 0 is in range.
findings.suggestion: Change the message to say "a value between 1 and 10000".
findings.confidence_reason: The branch condition and the retained message text are both present in the diff, and announce-step=0 deterministically exercises the mismatch.

findings.title: vcpu-dirty-limit error text still overstates the minimum
findings.description: The message updated by patch 2 still does not match the actual constraint. The code rejects only values below 1, so 1 MB/s is accepted, but the error says the parameter must be greater than 1 MB/s, which implies 1 is invalid. A concrete trigger is setting vcpu-dirty-limit=0: migrate_params_check() enters this branch and emits a message that misstates the accepted boundary.
findings.suggestion: Change the text to "must be at least 1 MB/s" or equivalent.
findings.confidence_reason: The condition and message are shown together in the patch, and the inconsistency follows directly from the `< 1` test.
